faqs.q: Is worthlessness a symptom of depression?
faqs.a: Yes. It's a recognized core symptom of depression, listed among the criteria clinicians use to identify the condition. When someone feels worthless, the feeling can seem like a simple fact rather than a symptom.

faqs.q: Does feeling worthless mean a person actually has little value?
faqs.a: No. Worthlessness isn't an accurate measure of a person's actual value. It's a symptom of an illness that distorts self-perception, much like a fever distorts how the body feels.

faqs.q: What if feelings of worthlessness come with thoughts of death or being a burden?
faqs.a: Treat it as urgent. In the US you can call or text 988, the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, any time, or go to the nearest emergency room. Support is available right now.
